QF.id: Issue Management - General Obligations and Due Diligence_59

question.text: Under SEBI ICDR Regulation 24, how old can the financial information in the offer document be as on the date of opening of the issue?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under SEBI ICDR Regulation 24, the financial information included in the offer document must not be more than 6 months old as on the date of opening of the issue. This is the SEBI standard for freshness of financial data. **The Trap:** 120 days is the requirement under Companies Act Rules (audited financials from prospectus date), not the SEBI ICDR requirement. 135 days applies to international marketing under Reg S/Rule 144A. Candidates must distinguish between these three different timelines. **Cross-Exam Context:** Financial statement freshness requirements are tested in NISM Series IX and also relevant for NISM Series XV (Research Analyst) when analyzing offer documents. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember the trio: SEBI = 6 months from issue opening, Companies Act = 120 days from prospectus date, International = 135 days from offer document date.
